seo.title: AI Agent Testing Strategies in Amazon Bedrock Agents Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai agent testing strategies in Amazon Bedrock Agents agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: AI Agent Testing Strategies in Amazon Bedrock Agents often fail due to the complexity of orchestrating action groups, knowledge bases, and guardrails in multi-turn conversations. Common issues include malformed Lambda responses breaking agent orchestration, knowledge base chunking missing critical context, guardrails failing domain-specific violations, and session state loss disrupting conversation flow. These problems manifest specifically through Bedrock's invoke_agent() API patterns and require targeted testing strategies.

howItManifests.scenario: Action group Lambda functions returning malformed responses
howItManifests.symptom: Agent returns 'Action execution failed' or incomplete parse errors in response metadata
howItManifests.impact: Breaks agent orchestration, causing conversation dead-ends and failed task completion

howItManifests.scenario: Knowledge base retrieval missing relevant documents due to chunking
howItManifests.symptom: Agent provides generic or incorrect answers despite relevant documents existing
howItManifests.impact: Undermines RAG accuracy, leading to hallucinated or outdated responses

howItManifests.scenario: Agent guardrails not catching domain-specific policy violations
howItManifests.symptom: Agent processes sensitive PII/financial data without redaction or blocking
howItManifests.impact: Compliance violations and potential data exposure in enterprise deployments

howItManifests.scenario: Session state loss between multi-turn agent invocations
howItManifests.symptom: Agent forgets previous context, repeating questions or losing conversation thread
howItManifests.impact: Poor user experience and failed complex multi-step reasoning tasks

detection.name: Lambda Response Schema Validation
detection.description: Detect malformed action group responses by validating against Bedrock's expected Lambda schema

detection.name: Knowledge Base Context Coverage Check
detection.description: Verify retrieved chunks contain expected semantic content using embeddings

detection.name: Session State Persistence Test
detection.description: Detect context loss by checking if agent references previous conversation turns
detection.code:
def detect_session_loss(response_history):
    if len(response_history) < 2:
        return False
    
    prev_response = response_history[-2]['completion']['message']['content'][0]['text']
    current_response = response_history[-1]['completion']['message']['content'][0]['text']
    
    # Check if current response acknowledges previous context
    context_keywords = ['as you mentioned', 'previously', 'earlier', 'you said', 'building on']
    has_context_ref = any(keyword in current_response.lower() for keyword in context_keywords)
    
    # Check for repetition of previous questions
    prev_question = prev_response.split('?')[0] if '?' in prev_response else prev_response
    is_repeating = prev_question.lower() in current_response.lower()
    
    return not has_context_ref and not is_repeating

fixes.name: Standardized Lambda Action Response
fixes.description: Fix malformed Lambda responses with Bedrock-compliant output format

fixes.name: Guardrail PII Policy Enhancement
fixes.description: Configure Bedrock guardrails with domain-specific denied/blocked terms

fixes.description: Maintain conversation state across multi-turn invocations

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Agent Testing Strategies Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex provides comprehensive test automation for Amazon Bedrock Agents by wrapping invoke_agent() calls with vex.verify() post-processing. This automatically detects all four common failure modes - malformed Lambda responses, knowledge base retrieval gaps, guardrail bypasses, and session state loss - using framework-aware assertions. Vex generates structured test reports with trace analysis and automatic remediation suggestions specific to Bedrock's action groups, knowledge bases, and session APIs.

The integration requires only pip install boto3 vex-sdk and wrapping your agent invocations. Vex automatically parses Bedrock response traces, validates Lambda schemas, computes knowledge base coverage using semantic similarity, monitors guardrail activation, and tracks session state persistence across multi-turn conversations. Failed tests trigger detailed diagnostics with exact failure locations in your action groups or configuration.

Vex's Bedrock-specific test suites run continuously in CI/CD pipelines, catching regressions before production deployment. It integrates with AWS Lambda test events and provides coverage metrics for your agent capabilities, ensuring enterprise-grade reliability for production Bedrock Agents.
vexAutomation.code:
import vex

from vex.contrib.bedrock import BedrockAgentTester
import boto3

# Install: pip install boto3 vex-sdk

bedrock = boto3.client('bedrock-agent-runtime')

@vex.test
def test_bedrock_agent_lambda_response(agent_id, alias_id):
    response = bedrock.invoke_agent(
        agentId=agent_id,
        agentAliasId=alias_id,
        sessionId='test-session',
        inputText='Execute action group'
    )
    
    vex.verify(
        'lambda_response_valid',
        response,
        schema={'completion.actionGroupsResponse[0].apiResponse.body': 'json'},
        required=['completion.message']
    )

@vex.test
class BedrockKnowledgeBaseCoverage:
    
    @vex.parametrize([
        ('Find customer SSN policy', 'ssn'),
        ('Retrieve pricing info', 'pricing tier')
    ])
    def test_kb_retrieval(self, query, expected_content):
        response = bedrock.invoke_agent(
            agentId=AGENT_ID,
            agentAliasId=ALIAS_ID,
            inputText=query
        )
        
        vex.verify(
            'kb_coverage',
            response,
            kb_similarity_threshold=0.75,
            expected_content=expected_content
        )
